data.title: Update project docs to reflect worktree isolation workflow
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Update existing project documentation to reflect the new worktree isolation workflow
**Why**: Future agents and developers need accurate understanding of the worktree dispatch, review, commit, and merge protocol
**Scope**:
- IN: CLAUDE.md architecture section, SUB-AGENT-BOUNDARIES.md (artifact isolation semantics, no-commit constraint, allowlist vs categorical block), WORKTREE-GUIDE.md (per-agent worktrees), dso-config.conf reference (worktree.isolation_enabled)
- OUT: Epic B (parallel review, Clash) documentation

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, CLAUDE.md architecture section describes the worktree isolation flow (dispatch → implement → serial review → commit in worktree → merge to session)
  ← Satisfies: "worktree.isolation_enabled config key"
- When this story is complete, SUB-AGENT-BOUNDARIES.md documents: (a) allowlist replaces categorical block, (b) sub-agents must not commit in worktrees, (c) artifact isolation semantics (ARTIFACTS_DIR resolves per worktree via REPO_ROOT hash), (d) review/test state flows through worktree ARTIFACTS_DIR
  ← Satisfies: "worktree-isolation-guard updated"
- When this story is complete, WORKTREE-GUIDE.md covers per-agent worktrees created by isolation:worktree parameter
  ← Satisfies: "Orchestration skills dispatch with isolation: worktree"
- When this story is complete, dso-config.conf reference includes worktree.isolation_enabled with documentation
  ← Satisfies: "worktree.isolation_enabled config key"

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] Follow .claude/docs/DOCUMENTATION-GUIDE.md for formatting

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Escalate to the user whenever you do not have high confidence in your understanding of the work, approach, or intent. "High confidence" means clear evidence from the codebase or ticket context — not inference or reasonable assumption. When in doubt, stop and ask rather than guess.
